channel.title: AI Unfiltered
channel.description: Chinese AI • Open Source • Security • Incidents. Signal, not noise.

item.title: ShowDoc RCE Flaw CVE-2025-0520 Actively Exploited on Unpatched Servers
item.description: A critical security vulnerability impacting ShowDoc, a document management and collaboration service popular in China, has come under active exploitation in the wild. The vulnerability in question is CVE-2025-0520 (aka CNVD-2020-26585), which carries a CVSS score of 9.4 out...

item.title: CISA Adds 6 Known Exploited Flaws in Fortinet, Microsoft, and Adobe Software
item.description: The U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA) on Monday added half a dozen security flaws to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities (KEV) catalog, citing evidence of active exploitation. The list of vulnerabilities is as follows - CVE-2026-21643 (CVSS score: 9.1) - An SQL injection...

item.title: UGREEN to Launch Power Banks Using BYD’s New Safety-Standard Battery Cells
item.description: UGREEN partnered with BYD Battery to develop power banks using new safety-standard battery cells following China’s updated regulations, which will take effect in 2027. The move focuses on improving safety under extreme conditions.

item.title: MiniMax Open Sources M2.7 Model with Broad Hardware Ecosystem Support
item.description: MiniMax open-sourced its M2.7 model with support from multiple global chipmakers and platforms, aiming to expand adoption in software engineering and AI agent applications. No pricing is involved as the model is open source.

item.title: Geely Unveils i-HEV Hybrid System with 48.4% Thermal Efficiency
item.description: Geely unveiled its i-HEV hybrid system with a thermal efficiency of 48.4% and fuel consumption as low as 2.22L/100km, among the highest for mass-produced engines. The system will be deployed across its core vehicle lineup.

item.title: LABBench2: An Improved Benchmark for AI Systems Performing Biology Research
item.description: arXiv:2604.09554v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Optimism for accelerating scientific discovery with AI continues to grow. Current applications of AI in scientific research range from training dedicated foundation models on scientific data to agentic autonomous hypothesis generation systems to...

item.title: Linear Programming for Multi-Criteria Assessment with Cardinal and Ordinal Data: A Pessimistic Virtual Gap Analysis
item.description: arXiv:2604.09555v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Multi-criteria Analysis (MCA) is used to rank alternatives based on various criteria. Key MCA methods, such as Multiple Criteria Decision Making (MCDM) methods, estimate parameters for criteria to compute the performance of each alternative....

item.title: Seven simple steps for log analysis in AI systems
item.description: arXiv:2604.09563v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: AI systems produce large volumes of logs as they interact with tools and users. Analysing these logs can help understand model capabilities, propensities, and behaviours, or assess whether an evaluation worked as intended. Researchers have started...

item.title: Turing Test on Screen: A Benchmark for Mobile GUI Agent Humanization
item.description: arXiv:2604.09574v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: The rise of autonomous GUI agents has triggered adversarial countermeasures from digital platforms, yet existing research prioritizes utility and robustness over the critical dimension of anti-detection. We argue that for agents to survive in...

item.title: AHC: Meta-Learned Adaptive Compression for Continual Object Detection on Memory-Constrained Microcontrollers
item.description: arXiv:2604.09576v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Deploying continual object detection on microcontrollers (MCUs) with under 100KB memory requires efficient feature compression that can adapt to evolving task distributions. Existing approaches rely on fixed compression strategies (e.g., FiLM...

item.title: The Diffusion-Attention Connection
item.description: arXiv:2604.09560v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Transformers, diffusion-maps, and magnetic Laplacians are usually treated as separate tools; we show they are all different regimes of a single Markov geometry built from pre-softmax query-scores. We define a QK "bidivergence" whose exponentiated and...

item.title: Fairboard: a quantitative framework for equity assessment of healthcare models
item.description: arXiv:2604.09656v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Despite there now being more than 1,000 FDA-authorised AI medical devices, formal equity assessments -- whether model performance is uniform across patient subgroups -- are rare. Here, we evaluate the equity of 18 open-source brain tumour segmentation...

item.title: Deliberative Alignment is Deep, but Uncertainty Remains: Inference time safety improvement in reasoning via attribution of unsafe behavior to base model
item.description: arXiv:2604.09665v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: While the wide adoption of refusal training in large language models (LLMs) has showcased improvements in model safety, recent works have highlighted shortcomings due to the shallow nature of these alignment methods. To this end, the work on...

item.title: Human-like Working Memory Interference in Large Language Models
item.description: arXiv:2604.09670v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Intelligent systems must maintain and manipulate task-relevant information online to adapt to dynamic environments and changing goals. This capacity, known as working memory, is fundamental to human reasoning and intelligence. Despite having on the...

item.title: Belief-State RWKV for Reinforcement Learning under Partial Observability
item.description: arXiv:2604.09671v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We propose a stronger formulation of RL on top of RWKV-style recurrent sequence models, in which the fixed-size recurrent state is explicitly interpreted as a belief state rather than an opaque hidden vector. Instead of conditioning policy and value...

item.title: Self-Calibrating Language Models via Test-Time Discriminative Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2604.09624v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are systematically overconfident: they routinely express high certainty on questions they often answer incorrectly. Existing calibration methods either require labeled validation data, degrade under distribution shifts, or...

item.title: Toward Generalized Cross-Lingual Hateful Language Detection with Web-Scale Data and Ensemble LLM Annotations
item.description: arXiv:2604.09625v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: We study whether large-scale unlabelled web data and LLM-based synthetic annotations can improve multilingual hate speech detection. Starting from texts crawled via OpenWebSearch.eu~(OWS) in four languages (English, German, Spanish, Vietnamese), we...

item.title: HumorGen: Cognitive Synergy for Humor Generation in Large Language Models via Persona-Based Distillation
item.description: arXiv:2604.09629v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Humor generation poses a significant challenge for Large Language Models (LLMs), because their standard training objective - predicting the most likely next word - inherently conflicts with the surprise and incongruity needed for comedy. To bridge...

item.title: Generating High Quality Synthetic Data for Dutch Medical Conversations
item.description: arXiv:2604.09645v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Medical conversations offer insights into clinical communication often absent from Electronic Health Records. However, developing reliable clinical Natural Language Processing (NLP) models is hampered by the scarcity of domain-specific datasets, as...

item.title: GIANTS: Generative Insight Anticipation from Scientific Literature
item.description: arXiv:2604.09793v1 Announce Type: new Abstract: Scientific breakthroughs often emerge from synthesizing prior ideas into novel contributions. While language models (LMs) show promise in scientific discovery, their ability to perform this targeted, literature-grounded synthesis remains...

item.title: Kuaishou Tests AI Coding Assistant KroWork with Remote Task Features
item.description: Kuaishou is testing KroWork, an AI coding assistant that enables remote task execution via messaging platforms, focusing on asynchronous development workflows. The tool is currently in early testing on macOS devices with Apple silicon.

item.title: SenseTime Launches Care U, Expanding AI from Vehicles to Home Scenarios
item.description: SenseAuto launched Care U, an AI home companion extending in-vehicle intelligence into household scenarios with cross-device memory and coordination. The product aims to connect car, home, and office environments through a unified AI system.

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Apr 14, 02:40 UTCUpdate - The Copilot Dashboard on the Insights tab is not accessible and we are continuing to investigate.Apr 14, 02:37 UTCUpdate - Degradation of Service - Insights PageApr 14, 01:57 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of impacted performance for some GitHub services.

item.title: Incident with Pages
item.description: Apr 13, 20:35 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved. Thank you for your patience and understanding as we addressed this issue. A detailed root cause analysis will be shared as soon as it is available.Apr 13, 20:32 UTCUpdate - We have mitigated the issue with Pages.Apr 13, 20:30...

item.title: Increased KV latency and timeouts
item.description: Apr 13, 20:15 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is aware of and investigating an issue impacting a subset of KV API requests, resulting in increased latency and timeouts. Workers using Assets are also experiencing errors. This does not impact KV through the binding.

item.title: JanelaRAT Malware Targets Latin American Banks with 14,739 Attacks in Brazil in 2025
item.description: Banks and financial institutions in Latin American countries like Brazil and Mexico have continued to be the target of a malware family called JanelaRAT. A modified version of BX RAT, JanelaRAT is known to steal financial and cryptocurrency data associated with specific financial entities, as well...

item.title: Disruption with some GitHub services
item.description: Apr 13, 16:59 UTCUpdate - We have identified the root cause and are rolling out a fix for Copilot. The services should now be in recovery, with expected full recovery in 5 to 10 minutes.Apr 13, 16:41 UTCInvestigating - We are investigating reports of impacted performance for some GitHub services.

item.title: Claude.ai down
item.description: Apr 13, 16:35 UTCResolved - We have resolved the issue resulting in elevated errors on login for Claude.ai and Claude Code betwen 15:31 to 16:19 UTC.Apr 13, 16:16 UTCIdentified - We have identified the issue affecting Claude.ai and Claude Code logins. We are working to apply a fix for this issue,...

item.title: Why opinion on AI is so divided
item.description: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. In an industry that doesn’t stand still, Stanford’s AI Index, an annual roundup of key results and trends, is a chance to take a breath. (It’s a marathon, not a...

item.title: FBI and Indonesian Police Dismantle W3LL Phishing Network Behind $20M Fraud Attempts
item.description: The U.S. Federal Bureau of Investigation (FBI), in partnership with the Indonesian National Police, has dismantled the infrastructure associated with a global phishing operation that leveraged an off-the-shelf toolkit called W3LL to steal thousands of victims' account credentials and attempt...

item.title: Booking.com Says Hackers Accessed User Information
item.description: The online travel platform has not said how many customers’ booking information was exposed, but said the issue has been contained. The post Booking.com Says Hackers Accessed User Information appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Wrangler logins failing due to scope error
item.description: Apr 13, 14:10 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 13, 14:02 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 13, 14:00 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare are aware of reports of `wrangler login` failing due to a scope error. This is affecting versions 4.82...

item.title: BrowserGate: Claims of LinkedIn ‘Spying’ Clash With Security Research Findings
item.description: Claims that “Microsoft is running one of the largest corporate espionage operations in modern history” face scrutiny as researchers analyze LinkedIn’s browser extension probing The post BrowserGate: Claims of LinkedIn ‘Spying’ Clash With Security Research Findings appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: ⚡ Weekly Recap: Fiber Optic Spying, Windows Rootkit, AI Vulnerability Hunting and More
item.description: Monday is back, and the weekend’s backlog of chaos is officially hitting the fan. We are tracking a critical zero-day that has been quietly living in your PDFs for months, plus some aggressive state-sponsored meddling in infrastructure that is finally coming to light. It is one of...

item.title: Want to understand the current state of AI? Check out these charts.
item.description: If you’re following AI news, you’re probably getting whiplash. AI is a gold rush. AI is a bubble. AI is taking your job. AI can’t even read a clock. The 2026 AI Index from Stanford University’s Institute for Human-Centered Artificial Intelligence, AI’s annual report card, comes out today and cuts...

item.title: OpenAI Impacted by North Korea-Linked Axios Supply Chain Hack
item.description: The AI giant is taking action after determining that a macOS code signing certificate may have been compromised. The post OpenAI Impacted by North Korea-Linked Axios Supply Chain Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: The Download: how humans make decisions, and Moderna’s “vaccine” word games
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. You have no choice in reading this article—maybe How do humans make decisions? The question has been on Uri Maoz’s mind since he read an article in...

item.title: International Operation Targets Multimillion-Dollar Crypto Theft Schemes
item.description: Law enforcement in the US, UK and Canada identified more than $45 million in cryptocurrency and froze $12 million. The post International Operation Targets Multimillion-Dollar Crypto Theft Schemes appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: CPUID Hacked to Serve Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads
item.description: Download links were replaced by a Russian-speaking threat actor to distribute a recently emerged malware named STX RAT. The post CPUID Hacked to Serve Trojanized CPU-Z and HWMonitor Downloads appeared first on SecurityWeek.

item.title: Job titles of the future: Wildlife first responder
item.description: Grizzly bears have made such a comeback across eastern Montana that in 2017, the state hired its first-ever prairie-based grizzly manager: wildlife biologist Wesley Sarmento.  For some seven years, Sarmento worked to keep both the bears, which are still listed as threatened under the...

item.title: You have no choice in reading this article—maybe
item.description: Uri Maoz loved doing his human research, back when he was getting his PhD. He was studying a very specific topic in computational neuroscience: how the brain instructs our arms to move and how our gray matter in turn perceives that motion.  Then his professor asked him to deliver an undergrad...

item.title: Elevated 401 errors for API endpoints
item.description: Status: MonitoringWe have applied the mitigation and are monitoring the recovery.

item.title: Workers AI Gemma 4 model unhealthy
item.description: Apr 12, 02:00 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 12, 00:29 UTCIdentified - The issue has been identified and a fix is being implemented.Apr 11, 21:52 UTCInvestigating - Cloudflare is investigating isssues with Workers AI Gemma 4 model. Users may experience elevated error rates when...

item.title: The inevitable need for an open model consortium
item.description: And yes, I hate consortia too.

item.title: Some users may experience issues accessing ChatGPT
item.description: Status: ResolvedAll impacted services have now fully recovered.

item.title: Email login down
item.description: Apr 11, 00:00 UTCResolved - Email login was broken between approximately 3:46 PM and 4:52 PM PDT on April 10, 2026.

item.title: [AINews] AI Engineer Europe 2026
item.description: Two quiet days in a row let us reflect on the first AIE in London.

item.title: Elevated errors on requests to Claude models
item.description: Apr 10, 16:51 UTCResolved - The issue affecting requests to non-Opus models has been resolved.Apr 10, 16:42 UTCIdentified - We have identified the current issue as affecting requests to models other than Claude Opus 4.6, and are working to fully resolve this issue. We will provide additional...

item.title: Increased HTTP 5xx errors in Philadelphia colo
item.description: Apr 10, 16:42 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 10, 15:56 UTCUpdate - We are continuing to work on a fix for this issue.Apr 10, 15:55 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare is investigating an increased level of 5xx errors in Philadelphia. We are working to...

item.title: Workers Assets increased upload failure
item.description: Apr 10, 16:26 UTCResolved - This incident has been resolved.Apr 10, 16:20 UTCMonitoring - A fix has been implemented and we are monitoring the results.Apr 10, 14:06 UTCIdentified - Cloudflare has identified an issue impacting Workers Assets uploads. Customers may be seeing an increase in Workers...
